papers.title: Paired CSF and Serum Proteomics in Multiple Sclerosis: A Welsh Neuroscience Biobank Study
papers.journal: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
papers.institution: Cardiff University / Welsh Neuroscience Research Initiative (WNRI)
papers.access_route: Welsh Neuroscience Research Initiative biobank / corresponding author
papers.notes: 157 participants with paired CSF and serum. Recent collection (2024 publication). Welsh Neuroscience biobank infrastructure. Subtype breakdown (RRMS, SPMS, PPMS) not specified in abstract. Strong candidate for recent-collection criterion given 2024 publication date.

papers.title: Matched CSF and Serum Biomarker Profiles in MS Subtypes at Dartmouth-Hitchcock
papers.notes: 67 matched CSF and serum samples from MS patients. 2021 publication. Smaller cohort. Access via corresponding author. MS subtype breakdown likely available in full paper. Recent-collection criterion marginally met (2021).

papers.title: CSF and Serum Multi-Omics in Progressive MS: A University of Nebraska Lincoln Study
papers.notes: 206 progressive MS + 46 RRMS with CSF, serum, and urine. Recent collection (2024). Large n for an academic MS cohort. Progressive MS overrepresented — useful for progressive subtypes specifically. Access via corresponding author.

papers.title: Serum and CSF Neurofilament Dynamics in MS: A Seville Hospital Longitudinal Study
papers.notes: 40 MS patients with paired CSF and serum. 2023 publication. Small cohort. Spanish hospital with potential access via ISCIII biobank network. Longitudinal design adds value but small n limits usability as primary source.

papers.title: Large-Scale MS Biobank at IRYCIS Madrid: 535 Participants with Paired CSF and Serum
papers.access_route: IRYCIS biobank / ISCIII biobank network / corresponding author
papers.notes: 535 MS participants with paired CSF and serum. Largest cohort in this bundle. 2025 publication — most recent collection. IRYCIS (Instituto Ramon y Cajal de Investigacion Sanitaria) has institutional biobank infrastructure. Access via ISCIII network or direct IRYCIS request. PMID not available at time of writing (PMC-only).

expected_retrievable_institutions: Cardiff University / Welsh Neuroscience Research Initiative
notes: Strong sourcing landscape for MS paired CSF+serum. IRYCIS Madrid (PMC12295370) is the largest at n=535 with a 2025 collection date — best match for 'recent collection' criterion. Cardiff/Welsh Neuroscience (PMC10874571) and UNL (PMC11586083) are also 2024 publications with solid n. Dartmouth-Hitchcock (n=67, 2021) and Seville (n=40, 2023) are smaller and older. Eval should check: (1) whether 'recent collection' is defined by collection date or publication date, and (2) treatment status — none of these papers confirm treatment-naive status in the abstract, which may be a gap for biomarker discovery studies requiring treatment-naive baseline.
